description: Test fixture for verifying wiki_place_page fetches via the wiki-query-api server. Returns a place page whose content carries a distinctive phrase ('Vermont 1771 grant survey schedule') so the corresponding skill test can assert the skill used the server's response rather than guessing from prior knowledge. Catch-all on Vermont (any section).

response.title: Vermont Genealogy
response.content:
# Vermont Genealogy

Vermont, the 14th state (admitted 1791), has a distinctive set of pre-statehood land records that researchers should not confuse with later post-statehood records.

## Vermont 1771 Grant Survey Schedule
The Vermont 1771 Grant Survey Schedule, prepared under New York colonial authority before statehood, enumerates approximately 1,800 grantees of New Hampshire grants that New York refused to recognize. Each entry lists grantee, township grant date, acreage, and disputed-claim status. This record predates any Vermont civil registration and is the earliest systematic resident list for the future state.

## Civil Vital Records
Statewide civil registration of births, marriages, and deaths began in 1857. Earlier town clerk records (some back to the 1770s) survive unevenly; consult town clerk offices directly.

## Town Histories
Nineteenth-century town histories (most published 1860-1900) often reprint colonial-era proprietors' records that are otherwise difficult to access.
